response: Both Ugni and Stenomesson are native plants of western South America, specifically Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and northern Chile. Ugni is a genus of plants in the myrtle family Myrtaceae, native to the Valdivian temperate rain forests of southern Chile and adjacent regions of southern Argentina. Stenomesson is a genus of bulbous plants in the family Amaryllidaceae, native to western South America, including Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and northern Chile.
